Tests the `isPrototypeOf` method to verify prototype chain relationships in JavaScript.

# Purpose
The code defines a series of constructor functions, [`Function_A`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_A>), [`Function_B`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_B>), [`Function_C`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_C>), and [`Function_D`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_D>), which establish a prototype chain. [`Function_B`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_B>) inherits from [`Function_A`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_A>), [`Function_C`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_C>) inherits from [`Function_B`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_B>), and [`Function_D`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_D>) inherits from [`Function_C`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_C>). An instance of [`Function_D`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/object-prototype-isprototypeofFunction_D>) is created and stored in the variable `d_instance`. The code then uses assertions to verify the prototype relationships within this chain and to test the behavior of the `isPrototypeOf` method.

The assertions check if `Function_A.prototype` is part of the prototype chain of `d_instance`, confirming the inheritance structure. Additional assertions test the `isPrototypeOf` method with various arguments, including primitive values and built-in objects, to ensure correct behavior. The code serves as a test suite to validate the prototype inheritance and the functionality of the `isPrototypeOf` method in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### d\_instance
- **Type**: ``Function_D``
- **Description**: An instance of `Function_D`, which is a constructor function in a prototype chain. `Function_D` inherits from `Function_C`, which in turn inherits from `Function_B`, and `Function_B` inherits from `Function_A`. This establishes a prototype chain where `d_instance` is an object that has `Function_D` as its constructor and `Function_A` as its ancestor in the prototype chain.
- **Use**: Used to demonstrate prototype inheritance by checking if `Function_A` is in the prototype chain of `d_instance`.
